notes: SHARED-RESOURCE TRAP. The DynamoDB table pipeline-states is written by three UNRELATED services that share NO service-call edge: ai-memory-svc, ai-scheduler and notification-worker. ai-scheduler deployed commit 6f8a0c2e at 07:26 which checkpoints plan state on every step, doubling its write rate (~210->480 ddb_write_rps) against the table's fixed 2000 provisioned WCU. ConsumedWriteCapacityUnits crossed 2000 at 07:31 and the table began returning ProvisionedThroughputExceededException to ALL writers, so all three victims' error_rate spiked together at 07:34. The page fires on ai-memory-svc (highest error_rate ~46%), but it is just the most-visible victim, not the source. The coupling is the shared table, visible only via the per-service datastores field in service_map.json and the table-level WriteThrottleEvents in events.json/metrics.csv -- not any call edge. Controls ai-inference-runner and platform-api do not write the table and stay healthy. notification-svc (off-table, postgres templates) deployed v44 near onset and emits benign HTTP 422 template errors at a flat rate -- an errored-and-shipped wrong answer.
_design_note: Paged on ai-memory-svc because it shows the highest error rate. Three services with no call edges between them fail in the same window only because they all write the same throttled table -- find the shared backing store, not a service-to-service cascade.
